title: Formula - subtracting TODAY from DOB to give current age
excerpt:
Hi, 
I am trying to determine the correct formula to use to say subtract today’s date from the date in this column (DOB) to give an age.

posts.author: McKennaOMG (McKenna Dorame)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

I am trying to determine the correct formula to use to say subtract today’s date from the date in this column (DOB) to give an age.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@McKennaOMG

The simplest way is to use the undocumented functions INT() & YEARFRAC():

INT(YEARFRAC({DOB}, TODAY()))

You can find out about other undocumented functions here: The monday Hidden Functions

Jim - The Monday Man (YouTube Channel)
Watch Our Latest Video: Column Total in monday.com Formulas? YES!!!

posts.author: McKennaOMG (McKenna Dorame)
posts.cookedHtml: @JCorrell One more question - is there something I can add into the formula to remain blank if there is no value in DOB rather than a red exclamation point?

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@McKennaOMG

IF({Date},INT(YEARFRAC({Date}, TODAY())),"Who are you kidding?")

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
